
## Reproduced analysis for GSE253742

```{r}

library(GEOquery)
library(utils)

path_save <- "/Users/alvise/Documents/uni/semestre3/laboratory_bio_datamining/data/RNA/expression_matrices"
```

```{r}
dir.create(path_save, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
supp_files <- getGEOSuppFiles("GSE253742", baseDir = path_save)
print(supp_files)

# List files we now have in the GSE253742 folder
list.files(file.path(path_save, "GSE253742"))
```

```{r}
# --- 2. Identify the expression matrix file ---
# Set `file_path` to the actual expression matrix filename you downloaded. If there
# are multiple files, pick the file that contains expression per-gene across samples.
# Example (update if different):
file_path <- file.path(path_save, "GSE253742", "GSE253742_FPKManno.txt.gz")

# If you don't know the exact name, run the list.files(...) line above and copy the path.
# stopifnot(file.exists(file_path))

# --- 3. Read the file (robust to comment lines) ---
full_data <- read.delim(file = file_path, header = TRUE, comment.char = "#", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
head(full_data)
dim(full_data)

# Quick summary of the numeric values to help decide whether data are counts/TPM/RPKM
numeric_cols <- sapply(full_data, is.numeric)
if (any(numeric_cols)) {
	numeric_summary <- summary(as.matrix(full_data[, numeric_cols]))
	print(numeric_summary)
	# check whether values look integer-like
	vals <- as.numeric(unlist(full_data[, numeric_cols]))
	integer_like <- all(abs(vals - round(vals)) < 1e-6, na.rm = TRUE)
	cat("Integer-like values across numeric columns?", integer_like, "\n")
	cat("Max value:", max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
}

# --- 4. Save as RDS for faster future loading ---
matrix_path <- file.path(path_save, "GSE253742_full_data.rds")
saveRDS(full_data, file = matrix_path)
```
```{r}
# --- 5. Load from RDS (example) ---
matrix_path <- file.path(path_save, "GSE253742_full_data.rds")
full_data <- readRDS(matrix_path)
head(full_data)
```

```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE FOR YOU: please confirm the expression unit and sample groups
# - Is the expression given as raw counts, TPM, or RPKM/FPKM?
#   Set the variable `expression_type` below accordingly ("counts" or "TPM" or "RPKM").
# - Provide the sample pairing / patient IDs and pre/post or condition labels.
#   Fill `sample_info_manual` below with your sample names, Patient IDs and Condition.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Example: set this to one of "counts", "TPM", or "RPKM". Update as needed.
expression_type <- "FPKM" # <-- CHANGE if needed

# -------------- Preprocessing & sample info --------------
# The sample groups are the following: 
# Naive: N1 N2 N3 N4
# Resistant: R1 R2 R3 R4

# Provide sample info here: replace the example below with your actual sample names/order.
# Example format: a data.frame with rownames matching `colnames(expr_matrix_raw)`
# and columns `Patient` and `Condition` (Condition should be factor with baseline first).
sample_info_manual <- data.frame(
    row.names = colnames(full_data)[-1], # assuming first column is gene IDs
    Patient = c("P1", "P2", "P3", "P4", "P1", "P2", "P3", "P4"), # example patient IDs
    Condition = c("Naive", "Naive", "Naive", "Naive", "Resistant", "Resistant", "Resistant", "Resistant") # example conditions
)

print("Please edit `sample_info_manual` in this script to reflect your samples.")
head(sample_info_manual)

# Quick boxplot to inspect distributions across samples
png("/Users/alvise/Documents/uni/semestre3/laboratory_bio_datamining/assessing_mutation_frequency_EGFR_NSCLC/RNA_data_analysis/images/boxplot_GSE253742.png", width = 1000, height = 600)
boxplot(full_data[,-1], las = 2, main = "Expression values across samples (raw)", ylab = "Expression")
dev.off()

# data is not log-transformed (large range), do a log2 transform for visualization/limma
expr_matrix_log2 <- log2(full_data[,-1] + 1)
head(expr_matrix_log2)
dim(expr_matrix_log2)
# add back rownames
rownames(expr_matrix_log2) <- full_data[,1]
dim(expr_matrix_log2)
head(expr_matrix_log2)

png("/Users/alvise/Documents/uni/semestre3/laboratory_bio_datamining/assessing_mutation_frequency_EGFR_NSCLC/RNA_data_analysis/images/boxplot_GSE253742_log2.png", width = 1000, height = 600)
boxplot(expr_matrix_log2, las = 2, main = "Log2(Expression+1) across samples", ylab = "Log2(Expression+1)")
dev.off()

```

```{r}
# ---------------- Differential expression ----------------
# Using limma for log-transformed data (TPM/RPKM)
library(limma)

# Convert log2 matrix to numeric matrix used by limma
log2_matrix <- as.matrix(expr_matrix_log2)

# You must fill sample_info_manual with Patient and Condition values.
sample_info <- sample_info_manual
stopifnot(!any(is.na(sample_info$Patient)), !any(is.na(sample_info$Condition)))

# Ensure factors and baseline
sample_info$Condition <- factor(sample_info$Condition, levels = unique(sample_info$Condition))
sample_info$Patient <- factor(sample_info$Patient)

# Filter genes: remove zero-variance and lowly-expressed
variances <- apply(log2_matrix, 1, var)
keep1 <- variances > 0
keep2 <- rowSums(log2_matrix > 1) >= 2
keep_final <- keep1 & keep2
filtered_log2_matrix <- log2_matrix[keep_final, ]

cat("Original genes:", nrow(log2_matrix), "\n")
cat("Filtered genes:", nrow(filtered_log2_matrix), "\n")

# Build design: paired analysis controlling for Patient
design <- model.matrix(~ Patient + Condition, data = sample_info)

fit <- lmFit(filtered_log2_matrix, design)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit)

# The coefficient name depends on the factor levels; inspect colnames(design)
print(colnames(design))
# Example: if your condition factor is Baseline="Pre", contrast will be ConditionPost
coef_name <- grep("Condition", colnames(design), value = TRUE)[1]
top_genes <- topTable(fit2, coef = coef_name, number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH")
head(top_genes)
```

```{r}


# ---------------- Check EGFR expression ----------------
# checking for EGFR expression
egfr_expression <- filtered_log2_matrix["EGFR", ]
print("EGFR expression across samples (log2 scale):")
print(egfr_expression)

# check how many times is EGFR present in the ID column
# --- THIS IS THE CORRECTED LINE ---
egfr_count <- sum(rownames(filtered_log2_matrix) == "EGFR", na.rm = TRUE)
# --- END CORRECTION ---

cat("Number of times EGFR is present in the gene IDs:", egfr_count, "\n")

```

```{r}
# construct the heatmap of the top 30 genes
library(pheatmap)
top_30_genes_ID <- top_genes$ID[1:30]
# include EGFR if not already in top 30
if (!"EGFR" %in% rownames(filtered_log2_matrix[top_30_genes_ID, ])) {
    top_30_genes_ID <- c(top_30_genes_ID, "EGFR")
}
# subset the expression matrix for these genes
heatmap_matrix <- filtered_log2_matrix[top_30_genes_ID, ]
#tail(heatmap_matrix)

# create annotation for columns
annotation_col <- data.frame(
    Condition = sample_info$Condition,
    Patient = sample_info$Patient
)
rownames(annotation_col) <- rownames(sample_info)
#head(annotation_col)

# plot heatmap
pheatmap(heatmap_matrix,
         annotation_col = annotation_col,
         scale = "row",
         clustering_distance_rows = "correlation",
         clustering_distance_cols = "correlation",
         main = "Top 30 Differentially Expressed Genes + EGFR",
         filename = "/Users/alvise/Documents/uni/semestre3/laboratory_bio_datamining/assessing_mutation_frequency_EGFR_NSCLC/RNA_data_analysis/images/GSE253742_top30_heatmap.png",
         width = 8, height = 10)

```

```{r}
# ---------------- Volcano plot (styled) ----------------
if (exists("top_genes")) {
    library(ggplot2)
    library(ggrepel)
    library(dplyr)

    # --- 1. Prepare the data ---
    res_df <- top_genes
    lfc_threshold <- 1.5
    pval_threshold <- 0.05 # Using P.Value is good for less-powered studies

    res_df <- res_df %>% mutate(
        # Create the 'class' column based on logFC and P.Value
        class = case_when(
            logFC > lfc_threshold & P.Value < pval_threshold ~ "+",
            logFC < -lfc_threshold & P.Value < pval_threshold ~ "-",
            TRUE ~ "="
        ),
        class = factor(class, levels = c("+", "-", "=")),
        
        # --- THIS IS THE CORRECTION ---
        # Gene symbols are in the 'ID' column, not 'symbol'
        gene_label = ifelse(ID == "EGFR", "EGFR", NA)
        # --- END CORRECTION ---
    )

    # --- 2. Create the plot ---
    my_volcano <- ggplot(res_df, aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(P.Value))) +
        
        # Add all points, colored by class
        geom_point(aes(color = class), alpha = 0.7) +
        
        # Add the text label for EGFR
        geom_text_repel(aes(label = gene_label), 
                        na.rm = TRUE, 
                        color = "black", 
                        box.padding = 0.5) +
        
        # Set colors
        scale_color_manual(values = c("=" = "grey70", "+" = "firebrick3", "-" = "blue3")) +
        
        # Add dashed threshold lines
        geom_vline(xintercept = c(-lfc_threshold, lfc_threshold), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey20") +
        geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(pval_threshold), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey20") +
        
        # Set theme and labels
        theme_bw() + 
        labs(
          title = "Volcano Plot: Resistant vs. Naive (GSE253742)", 
          subtitle = paste("Significance: Uncorrected P-value <", pval_threshold, "and |logFC| >", lfc_threshold),
          x = "log2 Fold Change", 
          y = "-log10 (Uncorrected P-value)",
          color = "Significance"
        )

    # --- 3. Print and save the plot ---
    print(my_volcano)
    
    ggsave(filename = "/Users/alvise/Documents/uni/semestre3/laboratory_bio_datamining/assessing_mutation_frequency_EGFR_NSCLC/RNA_data_analysis/images/GSE253742_volcano_plot.png", 
           plot = my_volcano, 
           width = 8, 
           height = 8, 
           dpi = 300)
}
```

## save the results as a tsv file for meta analysis
```{r}
out_path_742 <- "/Users/alvise/Documents/uni/semestre3/laboratory_bio_datamining/assessing_mutation_frequency_EGFR_NSCLC/top_common_genes"
# write to file the top genes object as a tsv file
write.table(as.data.frame(top_genes), file = file.path(out_path_742, "GSE253742_DE_results_with_symbols.tsv"), sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
```
